questions: Q1: Why does --repos reject repo names/keys with HTTP 400?
Q2: Why does --description silently use a file path string as the ticket body?
Q3: Is there an existing server endpoint for updating ticket descriptions, or does one need to be created?
Q4: Does the CLI already have a repo-name-to-ID resolution mechanism that can be reused?
Q5: What is the existing command registration pattern for new ticket subcommands?

answers.answer: create.ts:21 splits --repos values and sends them as raw repositoryIds with no client-side resolution. The server (ticket-service.ts:159-166) does a Map lookup by exact ID and throws HttpError(400) for any value not matching a known repository ID.

create.ts:19 uses requireFlag which returns the literal string from args[idx+1]. There is no file-reading logic, no --description-file flag, and no detection of whether the value is a readable file path.
evidence: src/lib/flags.ts:28-35 — requireFlag calls getFlag which returns args[idx + 1] directly

answers.answer: The server already supports PATCH /api/tickets/:ticketId with an optional description field via updateTicketSchema (max 10,000 chars). The patchTicket controller calls updateQueuedTicketForOrganization. Edits are restricted to DRAFT or QUEUED tickets (409 otherwise).

Yes. resolveRepo() in src/lib/resolve-repo.ts converts name/key/partial to repository ID via GET /api/inspect/repositories. It is already used by inspect logs, inspect db, and inspect api commands. Resolution order: exact ID > exact displayName (case-insensitive) > partial displayName. On failure, it lists available repos and calls process.exit(1).
evidence: src/lib/resolve-repo.ts:11-37 — resolveRepo function with full resolution chain
src/inspect/logs.ts:3,6 — import and use of resolveRepo
src/inspect/db.ts:3,6 — import and use of resolveRepo
src/inspect/api.ts:3,6 — import and use of resolveRepo

answers.answer: tickets/index.ts uses a switch-based dispatch pattern. Commands that need a ticket reference follow extractTicketRef + resolveTicket + handler. The new update-description subcommand should follow this same pattern (used by get, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle).
evidence: src/tickets/index.ts:56-65 — get: extractTicketRef(rest) + resolveTicket(config, rawRef) + cmdTicketsGet

statement: All three defects are CLI-side issues in helix-cli. No server changes needed — PATCH /api/tickets/:ticketId already supports description updates, and resolveRepo() already resolves names/keys to IDs. The root cause is that tickets create bypasses existing resolution infrastructure (resolveRepo) and lacks file-handling for descriptions. The missing update-description subcommand is a gap in the CLI surface, not a server limitation.
